task_completion.description: Agent opened and read the specification file to obtain key dimensions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window is open displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/tiger_moth_spec.txt'.

task_completion.description: Agent added a fuselage component to the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP Geometry Browser lists a 'Fuselage' or 'Stack' component, and a fuselage shape is visible in the 3D workspace.

task_completion.description: Agent added components for the upper and lower wings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP Geometry Browser lists at least two 'WingGeom' components intended for the main wings, and they are visible in the 3D view.

task_completion.description: Agent configured the vertical separation (inter-plane gap) and forward stagger of the wings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenVSP 3D view, the two main wings are arranged in a biplane configuration, with the upper wing positioned above and slightly forward of the lower wing. The XForm tab may be visible showing Z-location and X-location adjustments.

task_completion.description: Agent added a horizontal stabilizer at the tail of the fuselage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An additional 'WingGeom' component is positioned at the rear of the fuselage in the OpenVSP 3D view, acting as a horizontal tail stabilizer.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the completed model to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or the OpenVSP window title bar shows the file name 'tiger_moth_biplane.vsp3' and indicates it is being saved to the '/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenVSP GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenVSP application interface being actively used (e.g., Geometry Browser, XForm tab, parameter inputs), rather than just running a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Agent manually created and configured the components in OpenVSP.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show incremental progress of adding and modifying components in OpenVSP, rather than instantly loading a pre-existing completed model.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply write the .vsp3 file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The model is built within the OpenVSP graphical interface, and there is no evidence of the agent writing the .vsp3 XML structure directly in a text editor.
